summary: Given the acute presentation including proptosis and pain shortly after head/eye injury, combined with potential findings suggestive of an ocular bruit indicating blood flow disturbance near major vessels supplying or draining around orbital structures; Carotico-Cavernous Fistula emerges strongly supported by current diagnostic criteria correlating closely observed symptomatology patterns described herein against established literature reviews consulted during analysis process conducted up until present moment concluding phase. To reiterate succinctly without redundancy: Considering rapid development timeline alongside characteristic triad comprising exophthalmos/pain plus audible pulsatile sound within affected region implies high likelihood of CCF being responsible etiology warranting urgent evaluation via imaging modalities capable detecting anomalous communications between intracranial arteries & venous sinuses potentially leading directly into orbit causing noted complications manifest currently experienced by index patient under consideration at hand today per our thorough review above detailing pertinent
